text_blocks.text: Extension causes the rock units
as a whole to become longer and thinner.
This is primarily accomplished through
normal faulting and through the ductile
stretching and thinning. Normal faults
drop rock units that are higher below
those that are lower. This typically
results in younger units being placed
below older units. Stretching of units can
result in their thinning; in fact, there is
a location within the Maria Fold and
Thrust Belt in which the entire
sedimentary sequence of the Grand
Canyon can be seen over a length of less
than a meter. Rocks at the depth to
be ductilely stretched are often also
metamorphosed. These stretched rocks can
also pinch into lenses, known as boudins,
after the French word for "sausage",
because of their visual similarity.
